name: knox
description: Security enforcement layer for Claude Code. Blocks dangerous commands, audits every tool call, detects prompt injection.

preset.title: Security preset
preset.description:
How strict Knox is. Allowed: paranoid | strict | standard | minimal | disabled.
• paranoid — every block becomes an interactive 'ask' prompt instead of an outright deny
• strict — adds path protection + full sudo block on top of standard
• standard (default) — blocks pipe-to-shell, bash -c, eval, exfiltration; sanitizes sudo
• minimal — only miners + destruction + self-protection rules
• disabled — audit-only mode (self-protection stays on, all enforcement off)
Easier than this text field: type /knox:preset <name> in any Claude Code session, or run `knox preset <name>` in a terminal — both validate input and override this value. If you typo here, Knox silently falls back to 'standard' and surfaces a warning in `knox status`.

webhook.title: Webhook URL (security alerts)
webhook.description: Optional. HTTPS URL Knox POSTs to when a high/critical severity rule blocks something. JSON body shape: { timestamp, rule_id, command, severity, host, session_id }. Works with Slack incoming webhooks (https://hooks.slack.com/...), Discord webhooks, or any custom HTTPS endpoint that accepts JSON. Leave blank to disable alerts (default).

audit_path.title: Audit log directory
audit_path.description: Optional. Directory where Knox writes JSONL audit files — one file per day, e.g. 2026-05-09.jsonl. Each tool call is logged with: command, decision (allow / deny / sanitized), rule_id, host, session_id, timestamp. View with `knox audit` (CLI) or /knox:audit (slash command). Default: ~/.local/share/knox/audit. Leave blank to use default.
